reasoning: The US Corporate Bond effective yield has experienced a broader downtrend over the past 6–8 months, declining from approximately 5.38% in April 2025 to around 4.67% in late October 2025, before experiencing a minor bounce to 4.84% by November 7 (the likely anchor value for November 9's reference point). The official FRED tool statistical analysis projects a combined near-term probability of an increase at 0.49, balancing the recent bounce and seasonal factors against the prevailing downward trend. Because short-term momentum favors stabilization or a slight mean-reverting decline in yield along the recent trendline, I evaluate the near-term probability of an increase slightly below a coin toss (0.48). As instructed, uncertainty requires probabilities to be pulled closer to 0.5 as the forecast horizon extends, reflecting the unpredictability of Federal Reserve policy actions and broader macroeconomic shifts at the 1-month and 3-month marks. Consequently, the probabilities are monotonically moderated toward 0.5 over the subsequent dates.
